data.description: Workspace currently has product crates revdeck-cli/core/db/index/tui plus integration-test crates under tests/*.
CLI is a flat clap Command enum with a single match; M1 should add nested plugin subcommands here and delegate implementation to revdeck-plugin-host.
AnalysisRunRepository, ObjectRepository, and IndexRepository are the host-mediated DB commit surface for plugin runs and ObjectBatch acceptance; dry-run must avoid calling upsert methods.

data.name: analysis-run-backed importer flow
data.description: import_binary registers artifact/object, starts an analysis run, persists accepted objects/edges with source_run_id, records JSON diagnostics, and finishes succeeded/failed; plugin host should reuse this orchestration shape with validation before mutation.

data.naming: workspace crates use revdeck-* package names; core wire enums use snake_case serde names and as_str helpers
data.imports: domain contracts live in revdeck-core; DB adapters depend on core; CLI/TUI orchestrate rather than own persistence
data.formatting: Cargo workspace with separate tests/* crates for integration suites

data.summary: Place public manifest/ObjectBatch contracts in crates/revdeck-plugin-sdk and host execution/dry-run/audit logic in crates/revdeck-plugin-host; keep sdk free of rusqlite and keep CLI as a thin dispatcher.

data.issue: No explicit TOML/semver/json-schema validation dependency is currently declared in workspace dependencies.
data.impact: F-001 manifest parsing and revdeck_compat validation need explicit dependency choices before implementation; using transitive semver from Cargo.lock would be fragile.

data.naming: Rust workspace crates use crates/revdeck-* plus tests/* integration crates
data.imports: workspace.dependencies centralize shared deps; sibling crates use path dependencies
data.formatting: serde public enums use snake_case plus as_str/Display/FromStr patterns

data.name: clap enum CLI dispatch
data.description: Top-level Command enum is dispatched in main; ReportFormat uses ValueEnum and status-oriented commands print JSON summaries. Plugin commands should be a nested PluginCommand enum and may merit a plugin.rs handler module.

data.name: structured validation report
data.description: pre_export_validation accumulates serializable errors and warnings before returning a typed error. Manifest and ObjectBatch validation should follow this report shape instead of single string errors.
ObjectBatch SDK should reuse ObjectRef and StableObjectKey for stable identity; host validation can then bridge to ObjectRepository and IndexRepository without exposing private SQLite schema.

data.name: analysis run lifecycle and diagnostics
data.description: import_binary starts an AnalysisRun, records success/failure diagnostics_json/error_json, and tests corrupt artifacts for failed status and reopenability. Plugin runner audit should mirror this but likely needs plugin-specific states.

data.issue: M1 needs TOML/semver/schema validation and safe process execution, but workspace has no explicit toml/jsonschema/schemars dependency and no existing process runner or sandbox model.
data.impact: Plan must include dependency additions, permission evaluator, redaction/sanitized diagnostics, and transaction or validate-before-write guardrails for ObjectBatch.

data.issue: No product process runner, sandbox, timeout helper, child cleanup helper, or redaction module exists today.
data.impact: M1 must scope plugin execution as a safety skeleton unless a real sandbox backend is added; default-deny permission checks, output caps, env stripping, timeouts, sanitized diagnostics, and no-mutation failure tests are required.

data.description: ObjectRepository upserts objects/edges one statement at a time; ObjectBatch commit must first validate the full batch and, if commit is included, use an explicit transaction instead of partial writes.

data.issue: F-006 lifecycle states exceed current analysis_runs.status values and DB CHECK constraint.
data.impact: Plan must choose minimal diagnostics_json audit for M1 or add a plugin_runs migration; otherwise discovered/configured/queued/committing/superseded states cannot be queried reliably.

data.description: StableObjectKey normalizes component values, lowercases them, converts backslashes to slashes, and rejects final keys containing backslashes; plugin replay fixtures need Windows path and CRLF coverage to preserve determinism.

data.name: validation report before side effects
data.description: pre_export_validation accumulates serializable errors/warnings before rendering; manifest and ObjectBatch dry-run should follow this pattern and avoid DB mutation until validation succeeds.

data.summary: Deterministic replay must compare normalized graph bundles and accepted event digest sequences, excluding DB run IDs, wall-clock timestamps, raw absolute paths, and unsanitized process output.

data.description: analysis_runs is the current audit anchor but only supports running/succeeded/failed/canceled; plugin lifecycle should be stored in a new plugin_runs table linked to analysis_run_id instead of extending AnalysisRunStatus for M1.
ProjectDatabase exposes connection_mut, which is the practical entry point for transaction-backed ObjectBatch commit after dry-run validation; host code should avoid mutation repos during dry-run.

data.name: validate-before-render reports
data.description: pre_export_validation builds serializable errors and warnings before rendering; plugin manifest and ObjectBatch validation should return comparable normalized reports for CLI and harness assertions.

WorkspaceSnapshot already surfaces committed objects/edges via ObjectQueryRepository, but plugin run chips need a separate read model because latest_analysis_run is artifact-wide and could be polluted by plugin executions.

data.issue: No transaction-backed ObjectBatch commit surface exists; current import_success path writes incrementally through repositories.
data.impact: M1 must include validate-only dry-run plus explicit transaction/rollback tests before accepting plugin-produced graph facts.

data.summary: Keep M1 file ownership split by feature: SDK contracts in revdeck-plugin-sdk, execution/dry-run/audit in revdeck-plugin-host, audit schema in revdeck-db, thin plugin subcommands in revdeck-cli, conformance fixtures in a new tests/plugin-sdk crate.
